title: Add static Database Schema Viewer page

requirements.summary: Create a static DatabaseSchemaPage.tsx displaying 10 core database tables (Users, Doctors, Specialties, Doctor_Schedules, Appointments, Payments, Reviews, Medical_Records, Corporate_Accounts, Subscriptions) as hardcoded cards in a responsive grid layout, with no hooks, state, props, routing, API calls, animations, or dynamic rendering—only static JSX with Medical Blue theme styling
acceptanceCriteria: The file `frontend/src/pages/DatabaseSchemaPage.tsx` exists and exports a default React component.
The component contains exactly 10 table cards: Users, Doctors, Specialties, Doctor_Schedules, Appointments, Payments, Reviews, Medical_Records, Corporate_Accounts, Subscriptions.
Each card displays the table name as a heading and lists all field names with their data types as hardcoded static text.
The file contains no import statements other than React (and optionally React types).
The file contains no useState, useReducer, useEffect, useRef, useContext, useCallback, useMemo, or any other hook calls.
The file contains no function props, no component props interface, and the exported component accepts no props.
The file contains no .map(), .filter(), .reduce(), or any array/object iteration for rendering.
The file contains no conditional rendering (no ternary operators or && short-circuit rendering).
The file contains no routing imports or navigation logic (no useNavigate, Link, router, etc.).
The file contains no fetch, axios, or any API call.
The file contains no CSS keyframe animations or transition classes.
All card content is written as explicit, hardcoded JSX elements.
The page renders correctly in the browser with all 10 table cards visible and readable.
The styling uses Medical Blue theme Tailwind classes consistent with the rest of the application.

file_operations.description: Create a new static React functional component file that exports a default component displaying 10 database table schemas as hardcoded JSX cards in a responsive grid. Each card shows the table name as a heading and lists all field names with data types as plain text rows. The file must contain no hooks (useState, useEffect, useRef, etc.), no state management, no props, no routing logic, no API calls, no animations, no external library imports beyond React, no dynamic rendering (.map, conditionals), and no reusable sub-components. All content is written as explicit JSX elements. Use Tailwind utility classes consistent with the Medical Blue theme (jeevan-primary, jeevan-accent, etc.) for styling the grid container, cards, headings, and field lists to match the existing application design system.
